This test validates Akamai geo-DNS routing for a specific geographic region. When a client request originates from a given region, the Akamai edge DNS system resolves the hostname to the nearest Point of Presence (PoP). This test verifies that the resolver returns the correct regional endpoint, ensuring low-latency access for users worldwide. The geo-matching configuration is managed in the Akamai Property Manager under the 'Geo-DNS' behavior.

Importance: Incorrect geo-routing leads to increased latency, poor user experience, and potential SLA violations. Akamai's global traffic management relies on accurate geo-IP mapping and DNS resolution.

assertions.description: Response includes Akamai PoP region header

name: Test Cache Hit Ratio Exceeds 80 Percent
description: Verify cache HIT ratio exceeds 80% for static assets.
preconditions: Akamai property must have caching rules enabled with appropriate TTL policies. Origin server must be reachable and responding. Edge cache must be in a known state (purged or populated) for reliable hit/miss ratio measurement.
expectedBehavior: The response must include Cache-Control directives from the origin. Edge servers must respect the TTL and serve cached content for repeat requests. Cache hit ratio must meet the configured threshold. X-Cache header must indicate the caching tier that served the response.
documentation:
Verify cache HIT ratio exceeds 80% for static assets.

This test verifies Akamai edge caching behavior for the configured property. Akamai caches content at edge servers to reduce origin load and improve response times. This test checks that the correct caching headers are present (Cache-Control, Expires, ETag) and that the edge servers respect the configured TTLs.

Importance: Proper caching reduces origin traffic by 80-95%, improves global page load times, and provides resilience against origin outages. Misconfigured caching can lead to stale content or excessive origin traffic.

assertions.description: X-Cache header indicates edge caching status
Age header reflects cached object freshness

name: Test P95 Latency Under 200Ms
description: Verify p95 response latency is under 200ms for API endpoints.
preconditions: Akamai property must be deployed to all edge servers. Performance monitoring and RUM must be enabled. Baseline latency measurements must be established for the test region. Network conditions should be within normal operating parameters.
expectedBehavior: Response latency must remain below the threshold for P95 and P99 percentiles. Edge servers must deliver content with minimal TTFB. Performance must be consistent across multiple requests and regions.
documentation:
Verify p95 response latency is under 200ms for API endpoints.

This test measures Akamai CDN performance characteristics including latency, throughput, and TTFB (Time to First Byte). Akamai's globally distributed edge platform accelerates content delivery by serving from the nearest PoP to the end user. This test ensures that performance meets the defined SLOs.

Importance: Akamai's value proposition is built on performance acceleration. P95 and P99 latency metrics directly impact user experience, conversion rates, and SEO rankings. Consistent performance across global regions is critical for multi-national applications.

assertions.description: Time to first byte is under 200ms

preconditions: Akamai property must be deployed to all edge servers. Performance monitoring and RUM must be enabled. Baseline latency measurements must be established for the test region. Network conditions should be within normal operating parameters.
expectedBehavior: Response latency must remain below the threshold for P95 and P99 percentiles. Edge servers must deliver content with minimal TTFB. Performance must be consistent across multiple requests and regions.
documentation:
Verify regional latency stays within acceptable bounds.

Each region has a maximum acceptable latency based on distance
from the nearest edge PoP.

This test measures Akamai CDN performance characteristics including latency, throughput, and TTFB (Time to First Byte). Akamai's globally distributed edge platform accelerates content delivery by serving from the nearest PoP to the end user. This test ensures that performance meets the defined SLOs.

Importance: Akamai's value proposition is built on performance acceleration. P95 and P99 latency metrics directly impact user experience, conversion rates, and SEO rankings. Consistent performance across global regions is critical for multi-national applications.

assertions.description: Time to first byte is under 200ms

name: Test Strict Transport Security Header
description: Verify Strict-Transport-Security header is set with max-age >= 31536000.
preconditions: Akamai WAF and security policy must be active on the property under test. TLS certificate must be valid and deployed to all edge servers. Security headers (HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options) must be configured in the property manager.
expectedBehavior: The response must include all configured security headers. TLS handshake must complete successfully. WAF rules must allow legitimate traffic while blocking malicious payloads. Security headers must match the configured values in the property manager.
documentation:
Verify Strict-Transport-Security header is set with max-age >= 31536000.

This test validates Akamai security controls for the property under test. It verifies that the Web Application Firewall (WAF), bot manager, and security headers are properly configured and enforced at the edge. Akamai's security solutions protect against OWASP Top 10 threats, DDoS attacks, and automated bots.

Importance: Security misconfigurations can expose the CDN and origin to attacks. Headers like HSTS, CSP, and X-Frame-Options are critical for browser-side security. Akamai processes over 30% of global web traffic, making edge security a critical control point.

preconditions: Akamai WAF and security policy must be active on the property under test. TLS certificate must be valid and deployed to all edge servers. Security headers (HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options) must be configured in the property manager.

documentation:
This test validates Akamai security controls for the property under test. It verifies that the Web Application Firewall (WAF), bot manager, and security headers are properly configured and enforced at the edge. Akamai's security solutions protect against OWASP Top 10 threats, DDoS attacks, and automated bots.

Importance: Security misconfigurations can expose the CDN and origin to attacks. Headers like HSTS, CSP, and X-Frame-Options are critical for browser-side security. Akamai processes over 30% of global web traffic, making edge security a critical control point.

name: Cache hit ratio exceeds 80% for static assets
description: Validate Akamai edge cache serves ≥ 80% of static asset requests from cache.
preconditions: Akamai property must have caching rules enabled with appropriate TTL policies. Origin server must be reachable and responding. Edge cache must be in a known state (purged or populated) for reliable hit/miss ratio measurement.

documentation:
Validate Akamai edge cache serves ≥ 80% of static asset requests from cache.

This test is part of the A.W.A.R.E. CDN regression suite and runs against live Akamai edge infrastructure.

This test verifies Akamai edge caching behavior for the configured property. Akamai caches content at edge servers to reduce origin load and improve response times. This test checks that the correct caching headers are present (Cache-Control, Expires, ETag) and that the edge servers respect the configured TTLs.

Importance: Proper caching reduces origin traffic by 80-95%, improves global page load times, and provides resilience against origin outages. Misconfigured caching can lead to stale content or excessive origin traffic.

assertions.description: X-Cache header indicates edge caching status
Age header reflects cached object freshness

Importance: Protocol-level issues can cause interoperability problems with clients, proxies, and monitoring tools. HTTP/2 and HTTP/3 multiplexing significantly impacts page load performance.
